hypotheses.name: 3-phase sprint works across project types
hypotheses.evidence: THINK (plan mode with Gherkin AC) → EXECUTE (sequential build with /gate after each step) → SHIP (metrics, retro, baseline). All 3 phases completed cleanly for first Go implementation sprint.

hypotheses.name: Gates catch real issues
hypotheses.evidence: Build gate caught wrong interface signatures (pointer vs value CycleState/NodeInfo, string vs NodeInfo in Score). Fixed before tests were written. Without build gate, tests would have failed with confusing type errors.

hypotheses.name: Skills are followed by agents
hypotheses.evidence: 4/5 H7 audit instructions followed. Gherkin AC written (product-manager), tests for all source files (production-engineer), no premature abstractions (architect), minimal attack surface (security-auditor). Cheapest-model instruction not followed — all subagents used haiku for research, but Makefile/Dockerfile were written by opus in main session.

hypotheses.name: Flowstate works for greenfield projects
hypotheses.evidence: First code sprint on greenfield Go/k8s project. Produced working scheduler binary, 2 plugins with 100% coverage, Dockerfile, Makefile, all gates passing. Sprint 0 conventions and roadmap directly guided implementation.

hypotheses.name: Skills generalize across languages
hypotheses.evidence: Skills written for general use (not language-specific) applied cleanly to Go/k8s project. Production-engineer TDD process, architect module boundaries, security-auditor minimal surface — all relevant without Go-specific adjustments.
